title: Sudden Chest Pain - Spontaneous Pneumothorax
chief_complaint: Sudden sharp left chest pain and shortness of breath while playing basketball

physical_exam.chest: Decreased breath sounds on left side, hyperresonance to percussion

hidden_data.cxr: Large left pneumothorax with visible pleural line, tracheal deviation to right

key_learning_points: Tall thin males at risk for spontaneous PTX
No flying for 2-4 weeks after resolution

patient_responses.onset: It happened suddenly while I was playing basketball
patient_responses.pain: Sharp pain on the left side, worse with breathing

hints.imaging: CXR will show the pleural line

feedback.correct_message: Well diagnosed! This is a tension pneumothorax - a medical emergency.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is a tension pneumothorax. Look at the tracheal deviation and absent breath sounds.

cxr.comment: Diagnostic - shows pneumothorax and mediastinal shift.

feedback.attending_note: Tension pneumothorax: immediate needle decompression at 2nd ICS midclavicular line, then chest tube. Do not wait for CXR if patient is unstable.
